user_system_path: Post-audit #445: write the exact owner runbook for the paid value-lever run — the one test the audit cannot answer (does a real paid generation cycle surface a genuine gem, or still 'nothing cleared the bar'). Grounded in the verified code path: POST /api/settings/run-brief?use_llm=true gated by resolveSettingsRunBriefPipelineDryRun (PROD_DEFAULT_PIPELINE_DRY_RUN + ALLOW_PROD_PAID_LLM), bounded by hard caps (DAILY_SPEND_CAP_USD $1, MAX_MANUAL_DIRECTIVE_CALLS_PER_DAY 3, EXTRACTION_DAILY_CAP $0.25). Doc + ledger pointer only. Constraint: NO paid API calls (this just documents how the owner runs one).
allowed_files_raw: New owner runbook docs/OWNER_PAID_VALUE_LEVER_RUNBOOK.md + a one-line pointer in the keep-list ledger docs/SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_MAP.md + control-plane. Documentation only; no source change, no paid calls.
required_browser_proof: N/A — documentation; the run path (settings/run-brief + prelaunch-spend + api-tracker caps) was read end-to-end to ground every step and flag.
acceptance_condition: docs/OWNER_PAID_VALUE_LEVER_RUNBOOK.md gives an accurate, code-grounded, 6-step owner procedure to run one paid generation cycle with confirmation of paid_llm_effective, cost caps, kill switch, and the value verdict. Linked from the keep-list ledger.
anti_regression_checks: every flag/cap/endpoint in the runbook verified against current code (prelaunch-spend.ts, settings/run-brief/route.ts, api-tracker.ts)
stop_condition: Stop when the runbook is merged. Then the value lever is a clean owner action; between-rungs until the owner runs it or names the next seam.
next_command: Owner: run docs/OWNER_PAID_VALUE_LEVER_RUNBOOK.md (one paid generation cycle) and record in #445 whether a real gem cleared the bar.
